01-01.text: In 1863, the Emancipation Proclamation took effect in the United States.

01-02.text: In 1920, Isaac Asimov, a writer known for science fiction and science essays, was born.

01-03.text: In 1959, Alaska became the 49th state of the United States.

01-04.text: In 1809, Louis Braille, creator of the Braille reading system, was born.

01-05.text: In 1933, construction began on the Golden Gate Bridge in San Francisco.

01-06.text: In 1838, Samuel Morse first publicly demonstrated the telegraph.

01-07.text: In 1610, Galileo observed several of Jupiter's moons.

01-08.text: In 1942, physicist Stephen Hawking was born.

01-09.text: In 2007, the first iPhone was introduced, changing the way many people use mobile technology.

01-10.text: In 1863, the first section of the London Underground opened.

01-11.text: In 1935, Amelia Earhart became the first person to fly solo from Hawaii to California.

01-12.text: In 1966, the television show Batman premiered in the United States.

01-13.text: In 1888, the National Geographic Society was founded.

01-14.text: In 1973, Elvis Presley's concert Aloha from Hawaii was broadcast by satellite.

01-15.text: In 1929, Martin Luther King Jr. was born in Atlanta, Georgia.

01-16.text: In 2003, Space Shuttle Columbia launched on its final mission.

01-17.text: In 1706, Benjamin Franklin was born in Boston.

01-18.text: In 1882, A. A. Milne, author of Winnie-the-Pooh, was born.

01-19.text: In 1809, writer Edgar Allan Poe was born.

01-20.text: In 1930, astronaut Buzz Aldrin was born.

01-21.text: In 1976, the Concorde began regular supersonic passenger service.

01-22.text: In 1984, Apple introduced the Macintosh computer.

01-23.text: In 1849, Elizabeth Blackwell became the first woman in the United States to earn a medical degree.

01-24.text: In 1848, gold was discovered at Sutter's Mill, helping begin the California Gold Rush.

01-25.text: In 1881, Thomas Edison and Alexander Graham Bell formed the Oriental Telephone Company.

01-26.text: In 1788, the First Fleet arrived in Australia.

01-27.text: In 1880, Thomas Edison received a patent for an electric lamp.

01-28.text: In 1813, Jane Austen's Pride and Prejudice was first published.

01-29.text: In 1886, Karl Benz patented a gasoline-powered automobile.

01-30.text: In 1948, Mahatma Gandhi was assassinated in India.

01-31.text: In 1958, Explorer 1 became the first U.S. satellite launched into space.

12-21.text: In 1910, public libraries were becoming important community learning spaces.

12-22.text: In 1950, television was beginning to change how families learned about current events.

12-23.text: In 2000, the internet was becoming a regular part of school research and communication.

12-24.text: In 1890, inventors were developing new machines that changed daily life.

12-25.text: In 1970, environmental education became more common in schools and communities.

12-26.text: In 1980, personal computers began appearing in more homes, offices, and classrooms.

12-27.text: In 1990, digital tools started changing how people wrote, edited, and shared information.

12-28.text: In 1901, many schools and libraries were expanding access to books and learning for more people.
